violations.description: Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. Reach-in refrigerator on the cook line was found with an air temperature of 53F and multiple food items were found above 41F. Refrigerated units should be capable of maintaining potentially hazardous foods at 41F or below.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Cooked chicken, rice, and cheese sauce were found not dated in the walk-in cooler. Potentially hazardous foods should be date marked when held for more than 24 hours.

Temperature measuring devices. — --Temperature measuring device was not accurate or not easily readable. Thermometer in the cold hold unit for the salsa indicated it was holding at 50F. Salsa was found at 37F. Accurate thermometers should be used.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Blade for the chopper and the ice machine deflector were found dirty during inspection. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitize solution for mechanical warewashing machine was not maintained at the proper concentration. Dish machine chlorine sanitizing solution was found at 0ppm during inspection. Chlorine solutions for sanitizing food contact surfaces should be at least 50ppm.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. Onion and pepper mixture, macaroni and cheese portions, raw chicken strips and chorizo were found above 50F in the refrigerated unit on the cook line. Potentially hazardous foods should be maintained at 41F or below.

Person in charge. — --Certified food service manager had an expired certificate or is no longer associated with this establishment. Manager's certification has expired. Manager stated, via phone, that he took the class in February, but could not get the certificate. Please provide food safety certificate to verify successful completion of the class.

General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. Observed food employees putting on new gloves and changing gloves without washing their hands before putting on gloves. Hands should be washed before putting on new or changing gloves.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not properly protected from potential contamination. Pan of rice was found uncovered in the walk-in cooler. Food items should be protected from contamination.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Shelving units in the walk-in cooler were found dirty during inspection. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of individual disposable towels, continuous towel supply system, or heated air-drying device. Paper towel dispenser for the hand washing station in the server area was found empty during inspection. Paper towels should be available at all times. **Corrected during inspection.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
